Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.transformer

Interface ISimpleSequent

All Known Implementing Classes:
SimpleSequent
public interface ISimpleSequent
Common protocol for representing a simple sequent, without hypotheses management contrary to the sequent prover.

This interface represents a sequent reduced to just a list of hypotheses and an optional goal.

This interface also allows access to the formula factory and type environment of the sequent. Finally, some common sequent transformations are provided.

All predicates (hypotheses and goal) of the sequent are presented in a uniform way. The sequent is guaranteed to be reduced: It does not contain any true hypothesis or false goal. Moreover, if it contains a false hypothesis or true goal, then this is the only predicate that it contains (see getTrivialPredicate()).

Instances of this interface are manipulated by methods of class SimpleSequents.

Objects implementing this interface are immutable.

Since:
2.3
See Also:
  • SimpleSequents

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ISimpleSequent
    apply(ISequentTransformer transformer)
    Returns a new simple sequent obtained by applying the given transformer to every predicate of this sequent.
    boolean
    equals(Object obj)
    Tells whether the given object is equal to this sequent, that is whether the given object is another sequent made of equal hypotheses and goal, in the same order.
    FormulaFactory
    getFormulaFactory()
    Returns the formula factory of this sequent.
    Object
    getOrigin()
    Returns the origin of this sequent.
    ITrackedPredicate[]
    getPredicates()
    Returns the predicates of this sequent, together with a trace of their origin.
    ITrackedPredicate
    getTrivialPredicate()
    Returns the predicate causing this sequent to hold trivially, if any.
    ISealedTypeEnvironment
    getTypeEnvironment()
    Returns the immutable type environment of this sequent.

  • Method Details

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory of this sequent.
      Returns:
      the formula factory of this sequent

    • getTypeEnvironment

      ISealedTypeEnvironment getTypeEnvironment()
      Returns the immutable type environment of this sequent.
      Returns:
      the type environment of this sequent
      Since:
      3.0

    • getTrivialPredicate

      ITrackedPredicate getTrivialPredicate()
      Returns the predicate causing this sequent to hold trivially, if any.

      This sequent holds trivially iff either a hypothesis reduces to the false predicate or the goal reduces to the true predicate.

      Returns:
      the predicate causing this sequent to hold trivially or null if this sequent does not hold trivially

    • getPredicates

      ITrackedPredicate[] getPredicates()
      Returns the predicates of this sequent, together with a trace of their origin. In the case where this sequent holds trivially, this method will return an array of exactly one element, the one returned by getTrivialPredicate().
      Returns:
      the predicates of this sequent

    • getOrigin

      Object getOrigin()
      Returns the origin of this sequent.
      Returns:
      an object, or null
      Since:
      2.4

    • equals

      boolean equals(Object obj)
      Tells whether the given object is equal to this sequent, that is whether the given object is another sequent made of equal hypotheses and goal, in the same order.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - another object with which to compare.
      Returns:
      true iff the given object is considered equal to this tracked predicate
      See Also:
      • ITrackedPredicate.equals(Object)

    • apply

      ISimpleSequent apply(ISequentTransformer transformer)
      Returns a new simple sequent obtained by applying the given transformer to every predicate of this sequent.

      This methods detects whether the new sequent holds trivially. In that case, the new sequent is reduced to the single predicate that makes it holding. Additionally, predicates whose transformation becomes useless (true hypothesis or false goal) are removed from the resulting sequent.

      In case the transformer did no transformation at all and returned the exact same predicate each time, this method returns this simple sequent, rather than an identical copy of it. However, if the formula factory of the sequent changes, a new sequent built with the new formula factory is returned.

      Parameters:
      transformer - operation to apply to each predicate of this sequent
      Returns:
      a new simple sequent obtained by applying the given transformer or the same sequent if it was not changed by the transformer
      See Also:
      • ISequentTransformer
      • ISequentTranslator
